reasoning: Peer sheet: ~12 Denzi-style helmet/armor sprites in muted silver-gray palette, rich multi-tone metallic shading, moderate-high detail density, consistent 1px dark outlines, subtle highlights, cool-temperature palette with selective warm accents (red plume, gold trim), serious warrior charm register. Candidate (gem_emerald_v00): (1) Outline weight — 1px dark outline present, consistent. (2) Outline color — near-black, consistent. (3) Shadow direction — top-left light implied, broadly consistent with peers. (4) Shadow count — single shading pass, consistent. (5) Highlight intensity — FAIL: bright white sparkle highlight is markedly more intense than the restrained metallic sheen on peers; feels overlit relative to the group. (6) Color temperature — green/teal vs cool-gray-metallic; expected for a gem item type, not penalized heavily, but saturation level is an outlier even accounting for type difference. (7) Detail density — FAIL: gem sprite is visibly simpler and flatter; fewer distinct shading tones; peers show rich sub-pixel metallic gradients the gem lacks. (8) Proportion — acceptable for a small collectible vs wearable equipment; not penalized. (9) Rendering style — MARGINAL: chunky block-pixel construction vs the smoother multi-tone pixel painting on peer helmets; feels one generation simpler. (10) Palette overlap — minimal overlap (greens vs grays), but item-type difference acknowledged per rubric. (11) Charm level — FAIL: gem reads as cute/sparkly treasure aesthetic, peers read as serious combat-gear aesthetic; register mismatch would be conspicuous in an item grid. Overall: passes on outline and basic construction, fails on highlight intensity, detail density, and charm-register consistency. Marginal, not outright fail, because the core pixel-art language is shared.
